phases.summary: Morocco made the dream start, scoring after just two minutes and immediately putting Scotland under pressure with quick combinations and wide overlaps. Scotland’s five-at-the-back shape did little to contain Morocco’s movement, while their attempts to play out and counter were repeatedly disrupted, leaving Adams and others chasing long, loose balls. By the end of the phase, Morocco looked in complete control, with Saibari, Diaz and Hakimi repeatedly finding space and Scotland struggling to settle.

formations_blocks: Scotland defending deeper and struggling to establish possession
Morocco controlling territory with sustained pressure and overlapping full-back involvement
key_matchups: Hakimi vs Scotland’s left side/defensive cover, with Gunn repeatedly forced into saves
Hanley vs Morocco’s attacks and set-piece pressure
Adams being pulled back by Issa Diop
phases.summary: This phase is dominated by Scotland being unable to hold onto the ball, with Morocco keeping them pinned back and repeatedly exploiting space, especially through Hakimi on the overlap. Scotland do briefly try to gain a foothold, but their main tactical task is defensive resistance: Hanley clearing danger, McTominay heading away crosses, and Gunn making key saves to keep the score down.

formations_blocks: Scotland hanging on in a defensive phase
Morocco controlling the game and pinning Scotland back
adjustments: Scotland improved in the last five minutes before half-time
Suggestions in the commentary to bring on Shankland and Doak, but no confirmed change is described
key_matchups: McTominay versus Morocco's midfield control
Robertson’s passes/crosses into the Moroccan defensive shape
Scotland's attack versus Morocco's technically superior midfield trio of Bouaddi, El Aynaoui and Ounahi
phases.summary: The first half was largely one-sided, with Morocco dominating possession and territory while Scotland spent long spells hanging on for half-time. Scotland showed a slight late improvement, but the overall tactical picture was of Morocco controlling the midfield and Scotland lacking a clear plan or sustained ball progression.

Morocco continue to dominate territory and possession with their superior technique and movement
Scotland are trying to get up in the grill rather than sitting deep
adjustments: Scotland make no changes at the restart initially
Tierney goes off injured and Ben Gannon-Doak is added
Gannon-Doak goes in front of Patterson with McGinn shoved inside
McGinn is assigned to help watch Hakimi

Hendry vs Diaz

Scotland's defensive attention on Hakimi
Gunn vs Saibari/El Khannous from Morocco attacks
phases.summary: Scotland begin the second half by trying to press higher, but Morocco quickly reassert their control through Saibari, Diaz and Hakimi, creating the clearest chances of the phase. Tierney’s injury forces a reshuffle, with Gannon-Doak introduced and McGinn moved inside as Scotland look for a goal while trying to contain Morocco’s technical superiority.

phases.summary: This was a frantic late Scotland siege, with the hosts pushing Morocco deep and repeatedly getting into crossing and second-ball situations. Morocco responded by slowing the game, making late changes, and surviving a series of Scotland half-chances and a major penalty appeal before breaking out for a dangerous Talbi effort in stoppage time.
